content.problemStatement: Little chief has his own restaurant in the city. There are **N** workers there. Each worker has his own salary. The salary of the **i**-th worker equals to **Wi** (**i** = **1**, **2**, ..., **N**). Once, chief decided to equalize all workers, that is, he wants to make salaries of all workers to be equal. But for this goal he can use only one operation: choose some worker and increase by 1 salary of each worker, except the salary of the chosen worker. In other words, the chosen worker is the loser, who will be the only worker, whose salary will be not increased during this particular operation. But loser-worker can be different for different operations, of course. Chief can use this operation as many times as he wants. But he is a busy man. That's why he wants to minimize the total number of operations needed to equalize all workers. Your task is to find this number.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains an integer N (the number of workers). The second line contains N space‑separated integers W1 … WN, the salaries of the workers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of operations required to make all salaries equal.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: minimum salary is 1. Required moves = (1-1)+(2-1)+(3-1)=3. Second test case: all salaries already equal, so 0 moves.
Only one worker, no operation needed.

solution.approach: In one operation all workers except one get their salary increased by 1. This is equivalent to decreasing the chosen worker's salary by 1 while keeping the others unchanged. Therefore the relative differences between workers can only be reduced by decreasing values. The optimal final salary is the minimum initial salary because any larger target would require extra unnecessary decreases. For each worker we need (Wi - minSalary) decreases, and each decrease corresponds to one operation. Hence the answer is the sum of (Wi - minSalary) over all i.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case – one pass to find the minimum and another pass to accumulate the differences.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        salaries = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        mn = min(salaries)
        moves = sum(s - mn for s in salaries)
        out_lines.append(str(moves))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
